commands.summary: Renderer TypeScript build and esbuild bundle passed with the new Calls rail view, dialpad, call controls, and call types.

Renderer interaction coverage passed: 24/24 tests. CLV-015 coverage places and ends a voice call from the dialpad/contact selector and verifies the disabled state when public webhook configuration is missing.
Full Electron suite green after adding call UI: 77 tests passed and 1 opt-in live Twilio test skipped by design.

Visual smoke passed and produced Electron/dist/ui-preview.png plus Electron/dist/ui-calls-preview.png. The Calls screenshot renders the dialpad, disabled state, active status, recent call row, and end control without blank/overlapping UI.
Regenerated audits/reports/dashboard.md so Repopact reflects CLV-015 as satisfied with the new evidence run.
Repopact doctor reported the repository healthy after CLV-015 records were updated.
Repository governance validation passed with the CLV-015 work item and evidence metadata current.
Local system audit passed: required files, work item manifests, stale README checks, and markdown link/date checks are valid.
Production dependency audit completed with 0 high-or-higher vulnerabilities.

environment.process_note: 015 CLV-015 (call UI surface). ForgeLink now has a Calls rail view with dialpad, selected contact control, call/end controls, call status, recent call rows, provider disabled/configuration hints, keyboard entry/backspace/enter/escape behavior, renderer interaction tests, and visual smoke coverage. The UI presents call control and call history only; it does not imply shipped audio streaming, recording, transcription, voicemail, emergency calling, CNAM, STIR/SHAKEN, or E911 features.
